annotations.label: Actuarial science

anchor.snippet: Actuarial science is the discipline that applies mathematical and statistical methods to assess risk

annotations.note: Actuarial science as a discipline is not a mathematical object formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Present value of a future sum

anchor.snippet: referred to as the present value of a future sum

annotations.note: Grep finds no 'present value' or discounting/financial math definitions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graunt's cohort longevity patterns

anchor.snippet: predictable patterns of longevity and death in a group, or cohort

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no formalization of cohort survival/longevity statistics.

annotations.label: Halley's life table and annuity premium

anchor.snippet: Halley constructed his own life table, and showed how it could be used to calculate the premium amount

annotations.note: No life-table or annuity-premium concepts appear in Mathlib (grep for 'life table', 'annuity', 'mortality' yields no matches).

annotations.label: Commutation functions

anchor.snippet: "commutation functions" (essentially precalculated columns of summations over time of discounted values of survival and death probabilities)

annotations.note: Actuarial commutation functions are not formalized in Mathlib; the only 'commutation' hits concern category-theoretic shift commutation.
